 {"id":"bd-2vh3.5","title":"Tier 4: Auto-squash on molecule completion","description":"Automatically squash molecules when they reach terminal state.

## Integration Points

1. Hook into molecule completion handler
2. Detect when all steps are done/failed
3. Trigger squash automatically

## Config

bd config set mol.auto_squash true  # Default: false
bd config set mol.auto_squash_on_success true  # Only on success
bd config set mol.auto_squash_delay '5m'  # Wait before squash

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Post-Completion Hook
In mol completion handler:
- Check if auto_squash enabled
- Call Squash() after terminal state

### Option B: Git Hook
In .beads/hooks/post-commit:
- bd mol squash --auto

### Option C: Daemon Background Task
- Daemon periodically checks for squashable molecules
- Squashes in background

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completed molecules auto-squash without manual intervention
- Configurable delay before squash
- Option to squash only on success vs always
- Works with both daemon and no-daemon modes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","close_reason":"Won't fix - cleanup handled via molecule workflows (e.g. daily cleanup plugin)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.346152-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.797141-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.6","title":"Tier 5 (Future): JSONL archive rotation","description":"Periodic rotation of issues.jsonl for long-running repos.

## Design

.beads/
├── issues.jsonl           # Current (hot)
├── archive/
│   ├── issues-2025-12.jsonl.gz  # Archived (cold)
│   └── ...
└── index.jsonl            # Merged index for queries

## Commands

bd archive rotate [flags]
  --older-than N    Archive issues closed > N days
  --compress        Gzip archives
  --dry-run         Preview

bd archive list           # Show archived periods
bd archive restore <period>  # Restore from archive

## Config

bd config set archive.enabled true
bd config set archive.rotate_days 90
bd config set archive.compress true
bd config set archive.path '.beads/archive'

## Considerations

- Archives can be gitignored (local only) or committed (shared)
- Query layer must check index, hydrate from archive
- Cold storage tiering (S3/GCS) for enterprise
- Merkle proofs preserved for audit

## Priority

This is post-1.0 work. Current focus is on squash (removes ephemeral).
Archive helps with long-term history but is less critical.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210543-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

+{"id":"bd-313v","title":"rpc: Rich mutation events not emitted","description":"The activity command (activity.go) references rich mutation event types (MutationBonded, MutationSquashed, MutationBurned, MutationStatus) that include metadata like OldStatus, NewStatus, ParentID, and StepCount.

However, the emitMutation() function in server_core.go:141 only accepts (eventType, issueID) and only populates Type, IssueID, and Timestamp. The additional metadata fields are never set.

Need to either:
1. Add an emitRichMutation() function that accepts the additional metadata
2. Update call sites (close, bond, squash, burn operations) to emit rich events

Without this fix, the activity feed will never show:
- Status transitions (in_progress -> closed)
- Bonded events with step counts
- Parent molecule relationships

Discovered during code review of bd-xo1o implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:06:17.39523-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00","close_reason":"Added emitRichMutation() function and updated handleClose/handleUpdate to emit MutationStatus events with old/new status metadata. Added comprehensive tests."}

 {"id":"bd-396j","title":"GetBlockedIssues shows external deps as blocking even when satisfied","description":"GetBlockedIssues (ready.go:385-493) shows external:* refs in the blocked_by list but doesn't check if they're actually satisfied using CheckExternalDep.

This can be confusing - an issue shows as blocked by external:project:capability even if that capability has been shipped (closed issue with provides: label exists).

Options:
1. Call CheckExternalDep for each external ref and filter satisfied ones from blocked_by
2. Add a note in output indicating external deps need lazy resolution
3. Document this is expected behavior (bd blocked shows all deps, bd ready shows resolved state)

Related: GetReadyWork correctly filters by external deps, but GetBlockedIssues doesn't.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented filterBlockedByExternalDeps to check external deps satisfaction and filter them from BlockedBy lists","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-396j","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286971-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bsz","title":"gt mail send: support reading message body from stdin","description":"Currently gt mail send -m requires the message as a command-line argument, which causes shell escaping issues with backticks, quotes, and special characters.

Add support for reading message body from stdin:
- gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin  # Read body from stdin
- echo 'body' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' -m -  # Convention: -m - means stdin

This would allow:
cat <<'EOF' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin
Message with `backticks` and 'quotes' safely
EOF

Without this, agents struggle to send handoff messages containing code snippets.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-cnwx","title":"Refactor mol.go: split 1200+ line file into subcommands","description":"## Problem

cmd/bd/mol.go has grown to 1200+ lines with all molecule subcommands in one file.

## Current State
- mol.go: 1218 lines (bond, spawn, run, distill, catalog, show, etc.)
- Hard to navigate, review, and maintain

## Proposed Structure
Split into separate files by subcommand:
```
cmd/bd/
├── mol.go           # Root command, shared helpers
├── mol_bond.go      # bd mol bond
├── mol_spawn.go     # bd mol spawn  
├── mol_run.go       # bd mol run
├── mol_distill.go   # bd mol distill
├── mol_catalog.go   # bd mol catalog
├── mol_show.go      # bd mol show
└── mol_test.go      # Tests (already separate)
```

## Benefits
- Easier code review
- Better separation of concerns
- Simpler navigation
- Each subcommand self-contained","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:30:58.832192-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:42:49.390824-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:42:49.390824-08:00","close_reason":"Refactored mol.go into 7 files. Build and tests pass."}

+{"id":"bd-csnr","title":"activity --follow: Silent error handling","description":"In activity.go:175-179, when the daemon is down or errors occur during polling in --follow mode, errors are silently ignored:

```go
newEvents, err := fetchMutations(lastPoll)
if err != nil {
    // Daemon might be down, continue trying
    continue
}
```

This means:
- Users won't know if the daemon is unreachable
- Could appear frozen when actually failing
- No indication of lost events

Should at least show a warning after N consecutive failures, or show '...' indicator to show polling status.

Discovered during code review of bd-xo1o implementation.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:06:18.590743-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:16:04.64978-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:16:04.64978-08:00","close_reason":"Added error tracking with warning after 5 consecutive failures, reconnection message on recovery, rate-limited warnings (max once per 30s)"}

 {"id":"bd-d28c","title":"Test createTombstone and deleteIssue wrappers","description":"Add tests for the createTombstone and deleteIssue wrapper functions in cmd/bd/delete.go.

## Functions under test
- createTombstone (cmd/bd/delete.go:335) - Wrapper around SQLite CreateTombstone
- deleteIssue (cmd/bd/delete.go:349) - Wrapper around SQLite DeleteIssue

## Test scenarios for createTombstone
1. Successful tombstone creation
2. Tombstone with reason and actor tracking
3. Error when issue doesn't exist
4. Verify tombstone status set correctly
5. Verify audit trail recorded
6. Rollback/error handling

## Test scenarios for deleteIssue
1. Successful issue deletion
2. Error on non-existent issue
3. Verify issue removed from database
4. Error handling when storage backend doesn't support delete

## Coverage target
Current: 0%
Target: >85%

## Related
- Parent epic: bd-kyll
- Original issue: bd-7z4","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T13:08:37.669214532-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T13:08:37.669214532-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-d28c","depends_on_id":"bd-kyll","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T13:08:37.70588226-07:00","created_by":"mhwilkie"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ykd9","title":"Add bd doctor --fix flag to automatically repair issues","description":"Implement a --fix flag for bd doctor that can automatically repair detected issues.

Requirements:
- Add --fix flag to bd doctor command
- Show all fixable issues and prompt for confirmation before applying fixes
- Organize fix implementations under doctor/fix/<type_of_fix>.go
- Each fix type should have its own file (e.g., doctor/fix/hooks.go, doctor/fix/sync.go)
- Display what will be fixed and ask user to confirm (Y/n) before proceeding
- Support fixing issues like:
  - Missing or broken git hooks
  - Sync problems with remote
  - File permission issues
  - Any other auto-repairable issues doctor detects

Implementation notes:
- Maintain separation between detection (existing doctor code) and repair (new fix code)
- Each fix should be idempotent and safe to run multiple times
- Provide clear output about what was fixed
- Log any fixes that fail with actionable error messages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:48.411264-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:58.88609-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-z86n","title":"Code Review: PR #551 - Persist close_reason to issues table","description":"Code review of PR #551 which fixes close_reason persistence bug.

## Summary
The PR correctly fixes a bug where close_reason was only stored in the events table, not in the issues.close_reason column. This caused `bd show --json` to return empty close_reason.

## What Was Fixed
- ✅ CloseIssue now updates both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ ReOpenIssue clears both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ Comprehensive tests added for both storage and CLI layers
- ✅ Clear documentation in queries.go about dual storage strategy

## Quality Assessment
✅ Tests cover both storage layer and CLI JSON output
✅ Handles reopen case (clearing close_reason)
✅ Good comments explaining dual-storage design
✅ No known issues

## Potential Followups
See linked issues for suggestions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:06.887069-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-z8a6","title":"bd delete --from-file should add deleted issues to deletions manifest","description":"When using bd delete --from-file to bulk delete issues, the deleted issue IDs are not being added to the deletions.jsonl manifest.

This causes those issues to be resurrected during bd sync when git history scanning finds them in old commits.

Expected: All deleted issues should be added to deletions.jsonl so they wont be reimported from git history.

Workaround: Manually add deletion records to deletions.jsonl.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:48:14.099855-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

Default behavior shows all issues with a pin indicator.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:33:29.518028-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:30:01.484978-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:30:01.484978-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - --pinned and --no-pinned flags exist in bd list","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zc3","depends_on_id":"bd-0vg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:33:56.256764-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-zc3","depends_on_id":"bd-7h5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:34:07.486361-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zf5w","title":"bd mail uses git user.name for sender instead of BEADS_AGENT_NAME","description":"When sending mail via `bd mail send`, the sender field in the stored issue uses git config user.name instead of the BEADS_AGENT_NAME environment variable.

Reproduction:
1. Set BEADS_AGENT_NAME=gastown-alpha
2. Run: bd mail send mayor/ -s 'Test' -m 'Body'
3. Check the issue.jsonl: sender is 'Steve Yegge' (git user.name) not 'gastown-alpha'

Expected: The sender field should use BEADS_AGENT_NAME when set.

This breaks the mail system for multi-agent workflows where agents need to identify themselves by their role (polecat, refinery, etc.) rather than the human user's git identity.

Related: gt mail routing integration with Gas Town","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:46:33.646746-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.771325-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.771325-08:00","close_reason":"Not applicable - filed against stale bd v0.30.6"}

 {"id":"bd-zmmy","title":"bd ready resolves external dependencies","description":"Extend bd ready to check external blocked_by references:

1. Parse external:<project>:<capability> from blocked_by
2. Look up project path from external_projects config
3. Check if target project has provides:<capability> label on a closed issue
4. If not satisfied, issue is blocked

Example output:
```bash
bd ready
# gt-xyz: blocked by external:beads:mol-run-assignee (not provided)
# gt-abc: ready
```

Depends on: bd-om4a (external: prefix), bd-66w1 (config)

Part of cross-project dependency system.
See: gastown/docs/cross-project-deps.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:37:50.03794-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:42:25.042402-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T23:42:25.042402-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented external dep resolution in GetReadyWork - filters issues with unsatisfied external:project:capability deps","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zmmy","depends_on_id":"bd-om4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:38:38.106657-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-zmmy","depends_on_id":"bd-66w1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:38:38.175633-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
